title: Composer autoloading and version resolution
description: Composer resolves a version graph into composer.lock and generates a PSR-4 autoloader; apps pin the lock, libraries do not.
body: Use when managing dependencies or autoloading. composer.json declares version constraints (^ allows compatible minor/patch, ~ a tighter range) and a PSR-4 namespace-to-path map; composer install resolves them once into composer.lock and generates vendor/autoload.php which maps classes to files lazily. Applications commit the lock so every environment installs identical versions; libraries omit it so consumers resolve fresh. composer update re-resolves and rewrites the lock. Do not run update on deploy or hand-write requires. Actions: pin apps via the committed lock, run dump-autoload after autoload map changes. Validation signals include a clean install reproducing vendor/ from the lock. Risk boundary: resolving at deploy time runs untested versions in production.
